name: Cache Management for Faster Installs
description: Optimize npm install times by understanding, managing, and leveraging the npm cache for local development and CI pipelines.

steps.title: View cache location and info
steps.description: Find where npm stores its cache and check its current size.
steps.command: npm cache ls 2>/dev/null; echo 'Cache path:' && npm config get cache && du -sh $(npm config get cache) 2>/dev/null || echo 'Cache size unavailable'

steps.title: Verify cache integrity
steps.description: Check that cached packages are not corrupted.
steps.tip: This also garbage-collects unused cache entries, freeing disk space.

steps.title: Clean the cache entirely
steps.description: Remove all data from the npm cache when troubleshooting install issues.
steps.warning: Only clean the cache when debugging install problems. Clearing it means all packages must be re-downloaded on next install.

steps.title: Configure cache for CI
steps.description: Set a custom cache directory for CI pipeline caching.
steps.tip: Point npm at a project-local cache directory that your CI system can persist between builds. This avoids re-downloading packages on every run.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# npm Cache Optimization
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Cache Info ==="
CACHE_DIR=$(npm config get cache)
echo "Location: $CACHE_DIR"

if [ -d "$CACHE_DIR" ]; then
  SIZE=$(du -sh "$CACHE_DIR" 2>/dev/null | cut -f1)
  echo "Size: $SIZE"
  ENTRIES=$(find "$CACHE_DIR" -name '*.tgz' 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
  echo "Cached packages: $ENTRIES"
else
  echo "Cache directory does not exist yet"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Verifying Cache Integrity ==="
npm cache verify

echo ""
echo "=== Install Speed Test ==="
echo "With cache (warm):"
time npm install --prefer-offline 2>/dev/null || echo "Install completed"

echo ""
echo "=== CI Optimization Tips ==="
echo "1. Use 'npm ci' instead of 'npm install' in CI"
echo "2. Cache the npm cache directory between builds"
echo "3. Use '--prefer-offline' for faster installs with warm cache"
echo "4. Set a local cache: npm ci --cache .npm-cache"

faq.question: Does npm cache speed up installs automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. npm checks its cache before downloading from the registry. If a package version exists in the cache and passes integrity checks, it is used directly without a network request. Use --prefer-offline to prioritize cached packages.

faq.question: How do I cache npm packages in GitHub Actions?
faq.answer: Use the setup-node action with cache enabled: 'uses: actions/setup-node@v4' with 'cache: npm'. This automatically caches and restores the npm cache directory between workflow runs.
